evidence: The paper ranks variables by SHAP importance: "annual precipitation variability (PVA) contributing 28.6% of the total importance, followed by bacterial Shannon Index (18.6%), and sand content (9.9%)." This ranking supports the central claim that "climatic variability is the dominant filter on ARG abundance." However, impurity-importance bias risk is minimized because SHAP values (not raw Gini/MDI) drive the ranking, SHAP dependence plots show functional forms and thresholds, K-means clustering independently validates habitat partitioning, and spatial predictions are validated on held-out test sets (R2 = 0.60-0.86). The QRF impurity approach is mentioned only for spatial mapping, not mechanistic conclusions.
